Narrative_Meaning: Twos signify conflict, opposition and choices. They can indicate crossroads, partings, separations, and changes of direction.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Eelafin card is representative of the hybrid-faeries, who are characterised as having strong, graceful bodies, with just a hint of madness or wanderlust. Normal humans fear the Eelafin Doom-Weaving, that they usually imagine as curses. The Fae consider themselves half-doom and half-dream, the Eelafin they usually call Changeling, and consider them part-doom, part-doomed, part-dream, part-dreamer, and fear their Liberty and envy their mortality.

Significator.Plot: The Eelafin Plot is a hybrid itself, combining other types of Plots together to create something unique. You can draw for the hybrids, or simply choose. You might make a Romance-Detective Plot, where the "Who-dunnit" is as important than the "Will-They-Won't-They". Or maybe throw in a Masked Vigilante, who is also a serial killer...
Significator.Side: When sides of a Conflict are influenced by the Eelafin, they are marked out as being a hybrid, in that they are a half-elf, or cyborg, or something similar, or a changeling, meaning that sometime during the Loom (or during the Zenith) they may change, and reveal, that they were not what was assumed, as a Below Embodiment might reveal they are actually a Prince from the Above side in hiding. Sometimes the changeling will be revealed as being a single Embodiment within a side, to the rest of that side, during the Frame. They are then trying to keep this information secret from the other sides for the rest of the Plot (until the Zenith Final Revelation ideally).
Significator.Character:
The Eelafin Significator indicates a Character who seems at odds with themselves.

They Gain Yin when they act fatalistic, pessimistic, or depressed.

They Gain Yang when they act optimistic, positive, or excited.

They Gain Chi when they day-dream, or deal with, or talk about, a situation as it really is.

They gain +1 Success Level to the separate actions when they perform two or more at a time (shoot two or three targets, shoot while somersaulting, etc).
Significator.Scene: The Eelafin Scene is a Scene that straddles two worlds at the same time. This is not the same as a portal or threshold that joins two places with a liminal space. Here the Scene blends the two worlds together, like the recreation of your bedroom deep within the dream. Yarn-Tellers may play the card to create a Scene that blends the real world with another world (often a Quantum Alternate), perhaps in the form of a model, book, or piece of art or technology from that world.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching specifically the lines that change state between their First and Second Hexagram. The 3rd changing Line of The Creative for example rewards caution and responsibility, allowing the Hook to offer a situation where these may be demonstrated.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Eelafin are known not just for their Doom-sense, but for their heightened senses in general. Perhaps a cry for help is heard, or a crime is witnessed by the Character to Hook them.
Yarn.Gain: Bloodline/Doom Descendant (some bloodlines and destinies grant extra Skills, Talents or even Powers, the Character discovers one of theirs)
Yarn.Fray_Description: A Doom is a profound, Chronolithed event, a predestination paradox, or an event that is part of history and should not be changed, they are always Paradoxical Stakes Ordeals. Dooms are sensed approaching by every Mercari, Eelafin, or Yarn-Teller present. While a Doom is influenced and affected by the cards played, it is only the Ordeal cards that define the final success of the Doom, be careful, you may find yourself on the wrong side of history.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Fray is complicated by a situation that involved many of the PCs Hitches. If a Character has a Woe that hasn't been triggered recently, now is the time. If they have a a few quirks, find a scenario that triggers them all, sequentially or together. For fairness, every Character involved in the Fray should have at least one of their Hitches triggered regardless of side.

NPC.Description: Those who spend too much time around the Fae become different, usually indifferent to mortal rules and morals, their incredible experiences often leave them mad, but gifted with some ability most mortals require years of dedicated practice to learn.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text:
Destiny sounds like a simple thing, but what it often means is years of study, months of rigorous training, and at least one Crisis. This is one of those events, Gain, as appropriate
  • New Proficiencies and a new Skill Annex
  • Increase an Annex one Tier, gaining Proficiencies as required.
  • A new Skill/Talent/Power Learning, Training, or Lore Descendant
  • A new Monolith Descendant
Yarn.History_Description: The Eelafin indicates that the current situation has been caused by interaction between two very disparate forces. One side is usually mundane, often ordinary people and the other is usually other-worldly, or super-, preter- or just un-natural such as Færies, Increated, Eusaiveans or Toons.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Eelafin indicates that the current situation has somebody who has been replaced, this may be with an actor, a mole, a clone, alien puppet-master, a magical realism replacement such as a shape-shifter, face-dancer, doppelganger, or an actual changeling, or a more supernatural explanation, such as a spirit, angelic or demonic possession or something more weird like a Eusaivean deep cover agent. The replacement may act as completely differently, or more interestingly they may behave almost perfectly the same as the original.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Eelafin indicates that the solution to the current situation is to combine at least two potential, but often unlikely, solutions together to synthesise a new solution that will probably work.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, all the sides and anything in between. It is normal when providing information to always reveal information about the Dominant Side as well as any others.
Revelations.Info_Description: Suspicions vary in scope and accuracy more than any other Information. If a detective suspects some one of a crime, they are probably right more often than a conspiracy theorist is right about the government, but both can be wrong, or ultimately proved right. Suspicions lack evidence, and are often little more than a reaction to a person or group.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is present upon a tapestry, poster, billboard, or perhaps is read from Wyrd's Tapestry directly, by Doom-reading from cards in play "The bad guy has a Wyrd Tarot Hand?! He must be at least a Mercari.", "She negated a Yarn card?! Run! Flee with extra flee!"
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is specifically about the powers the Embodiment has, whether Annexes or political power and pacts.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Peculiar details are odd little out-of-place touches that point to something bigger, or more extraordinary than you might first have guessed. They are the footprints that stop in the middle of the field, or that pass through (or over) a solid wall.

Obstacle.Description: The ground ahead is unstable, it might be loose rocks, scree, rubble, or fallen logs, who knows. You'll have to be careful and have great balance, though. It looks like the kind of place that people say, "...and that's how I lost my leg" about.

Tarot.Normal: Automatic Proficiency Crisis. Add a Proficiency to an Annex (or merge two Proficiencies into a Skill).

Ordeal.Stage_Description: The route ahead is cluttered, perhaps with parked cards, litter bins. Book stacks, filing cabinets, shelves, scaffolding poles, pipes, trees or bamboo poles can also create clutter.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Who is that ahead of you? You need to catch them to find out. Drop a gear and put your foot to the floor.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Ebbing enemy position is good, they are in the best place to control the battle, the only issue is the Flowing forces are massing to test these defences. Defend hard enough and the Tide will turn. Defend any Attack and you can retaliate with a Personality Annex Psych Attack as a free action.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The fight mostly consists of watching, and waiting. Occasionally a solitary usually Vex or Chorus Extra Opponent will test your defences, very occasionally something bigger will come.
  Facet    Monster  
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: Microscopic animal and algal plant and bacterial paths, often overlooked.

Stress.Description: Worries make a Character think about their issues, dwelling on what questions or issues are stressing them out.
Stress.Rules: Character may reduce their Stress by 1 for each statement that relates to their worries that they make, or each Clubs Pip Played on Psycho-social Actions that relate to their worries, or they may pay Chi equal to their current Stress to remove 1 Stress, 1 Strain or 1 Shock.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can create delusions, hallucinations, waking dreams, and strange beliefs. At one time, those who were touched by such madness were considered blessed, or cursed, by the Fae, and many suspected that there was some deeper truth to this madness. There are stil lingering traces of this, with fairies associated with psychedelic mushrooms, dreamstates and nonsense fantasy.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma creates a "Faery-Delusion", these are powerful delusions that may or may not be actually connected with Færy (the other other world of the Fæ). If the Character is genuinely seeing Fairy beings around them, or spending the night in Færy to return tired to ouur world in the morning, then trying to convey these stories will cause the Character to instead speak nonsense. Otherwise speaking nonsense, pointing out imaginary or hallucinatory beings, or holding one-sided conversations with an imaginary friend, will Relieve 1 Stress.

Age.Description: An Age of Jumbling is occurring. During this age the society encounters and merges with another society. This intermingling of societies causes both to grow and approach the other culturally. Societies often gain or swap proficiencies, technologies, artistic styles, words or grammatical peculiarities, as well as creating a new culture during this time.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Jumbling a society blends itself with at least one other, essentially creating a new culture within the society, this new culture may later move to dominate the society, or may later be rejected by the spciety as a whole.
